title: Understanding the modern cybercrime landscape
optimized_headline: Exploring Today’s Evolving Cybercrime Landscape: Key Trends and Insights
raw_excerpt: Throughout 2025, HPE observed significant changes in how cybercriminals operate. Analyzing real-world threats, our HPE Threat Labs highlighted an industrialization of the cyber criminals’ methods in its new In the Wild Report, enabling greater scale, speed and structure in their campaigns. They typically use automation and AI to exploit longstanding vulnerabilities, and many have adopted…
raw_body: Throughout 2025, HPE observed significant changes in how cybercriminals operate. Analyzing real-world threats, our HPE Threat Labs highlighted an industrialization of the cyber criminals’ methods in its new In the Wild Report, enabling greater scale, speed and structure in their campaigns. They typically use automation and AI to exploit longstanding vulnerabilities, and many have adopted…
speedrun: In 2025, HPE's Threat Labs reported a marked shift in cybercrime tactics, revealing that criminals are increasingly industrializing their operations. They leverage automation and AI to exploit existing vulnerabilities, allowing for faster and more organized attacks. This trend highlights a growing sophistication in cyber threats. As these methods evolve, understanding them becomes crucial for businesses and individuals alike.
why_it_matters: Businesses face immediate risks as cybercriminals can now execute attacks more efficiently, potentially leading to significant data breaches.
This shift indicates a broader trend in the cybercrime landscape, emphasizing the need for enhanced security measures across industries.

lenses.eli12: Cybercriminals are getting smarter and faster, using tools like AI to launch attacks. Imagine a factory that churns out products at lightning speed; that's how these criminals are now operating. This matters because it means everyone, from regular users to big companies, needs to be more vigilant about online security.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, the rise of sophisticated cybercrime means they must prioritize security features in their products. With criminals using automation, the cost of breaches could rise significantly. This shift could lead to increased demand for robust security solutions, impacting product development and customer trust.
lenses.engineer: The report indicates that cybercriminals are employing advanced automation and AI techniques to enhance their attack strategies. By exploiting existing vulnerabilities at scale, they can execute more complex operations rapidly. This trend necessitates a reevaluation of current security frameworks to address these evolving threats effectively.
